# UI-127 — Foundation shell design tokens

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Extract the desktop-shell layout/colour tokens that every module shell uses in the prototype into a single Tailwind v4 globals.css/CSS-variable source of truth, so UI-129/130/131/132/T-134 cannot drift from each other.

## Source of truth (prototype anchors)
Look at how these prototype `shell.jsx` files style sidebar (~280px), topbar (~56px), settings subnav (~240px), scanner frame (390×844). Reconcile any inter-module differences; if a width or colour conflicts, use `settings/shell.jsx` values and document the deviation:

- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:1-105`
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/shell.jsx:1-74`
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/production/shell.jsx` (sidebar/topbar)
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/warehouse/shell.jsx` (PSidebar)
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/multi-site/shell.jsx` (MsTopbar with site-switch slot)
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/oee/shell.jsx` (PageHead)
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/scanner/shell.jsx:1-66` (ScannerFrame)

The brief is `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md` §2.

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/app/globals.css` [modify — Tailwind v4 source of truth: append shell CSS variables and expose theme tokens via `@theme { ... }` and/or CSS-variable-backed utilities; do NOT add or require a Tailwind config file]
- `apps/web/app/_components/__tests__/shell-tokens.test.tsx` [create — RTL contract test that renders a fixture using each utility class and asserts `getComputedStyle().<property>` matches the declared px/colour]

Do NOT modify `proxy.ts`, `[locale]/layout.tsx`, `[locale]/login/layout.tsx`, or any task-T-XXX.json file outside this wave.

## Token contract
Tokens (CSS variables on `:root`) — exact values come from the prototype, transcribed:

- `--shell-sidebar-w: 280px`
- `--shell-subnav-w: 240px`
- `--shell-topbar-h: 56px`
- `--shell-scanner-w: 390px`
- `--shell-scanner-h: 844px`
- `--shell-bg`, `--shell-fg`, `--shell-border`, `--shell-muted`, `--shell-active`, `--shell-active-fg`, `--shell-badge-blue` (HSL values from the prototype palette; cite the rgb/hex lines from `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/_tokens/*.css` if present, else use the `style="…"` literals from `settings/shell.jsx`).

Each token must be exposed both as CSS var and as a Tailwind utility (e.g. `w-sidebar`, `h-topbar`, `text-shell-muted`, `bg-shell-active`).

## Acceptance criteria
1. `apps/web/app/globals.css` defines all `--shell-*` tokens in `@layer base`.
2. `apps/web/app/globals.css` exposes corresponding utilities; `pnpm --filter web lint` and `typecheck` pass.
3. `apps/web/app/_components/__tests__/shell-tokens.test.tsx` asserts each utility produces the expected computed style, and the test passes (`pnpm --filter web test shell-tokens`).
4. No existing screen visual changes — `app/[locale]/login/layout.tsx`, `app/[locale]/page.tsx`, and `(admin)/settings/*` rendered output is byte-identical (no token used yet by them) — verified by running the existing `pnpm --filter web test` suite before/after.
5. Deviations from any inter-module prototype mismatch documented in PR/closeout.

## Out of scope
- Building sidebar/topbar components (UI-129/130).
- Touching `packages/ui/` primitives (PageHeader lands in UI-132).
- Mobile/responsive breakpoints (deferred wave).

## RED test
Create `apps/web/app/_components/__tests__/shell-tokens.test.tsx` before implementation — it must fail because the utilities/vars are absent, then pass after the implementation.

## Risk red lines
- DO NOT inline px values inside shell components — every UI task in this wave must consume these tokens.
- DO NOT introduce a new colour family. Stay on the prototype palette.
- DO NOT modify `apps/web/proxy.ts`.

## Closeout
Provide: screenshots of the token fixture rendered in dev (`/_dev/shell-tokens` ad-hoc page or Vitest DOM snapshot), screenshot_or_dom_diff_artifacts, visual_parity_evidence (cite prototype line ranges), prototype_reference, console_network_check (no console errors), acceptance_criteria_status, test_commands_and_results, changed_files, git_status, deviations_from_prototype.

If parity evidence cannot be captured, the task is BLOCKED / needs_human — not completed.

# UI-128 — Foundation navigation manifest

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Provide a single typed source of truth for the desktop AppSidebar groups/items and for the Settings sub-nav, so UI-129 and UI-132 do not duplicate copy or hard-code routes. This is a data/contract task — no JSX, no styling.

## Source of truth
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:3-21` (`SSidebar`): groups Core / Operations / QA & Shipping / Premium.
- Cross-ref the active-item placement in every `<module>/shell.jsx` to confirm the same item list across modules (settings, technical, production, warehouse, planning, quality, shipping, finance, oee, maintenance).
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:35-59` + the `window.SETTINGS_NAV` array referenced there (search prototypes/data*.jsx if needed).
- Brief: `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md` §1 (snapshot), §3 Luka B/E.

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/app-nav.ts` [create — exports `APP_NAV_GROUPS: AppNavGroup[]`]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/settings-nav.ts` [create — exports `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS: SettingsNavGroup[]`]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/types.ts` [create — `AppNavGroup`, `AppNavItem`, `SettingsNavGroup`, `SettingsNavItem` with `i18n_key`, `route`, `icon_token`, `count_slot` (always `null` here), `permission_key` (always `null` here), `rbac_todo` flag, `highlight` flag]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/__tests__/app-nav.test.ts` [create]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/__tests__/settings-nav.test.ts` [create]
- `apps/web/i18n/en.json` [modify — add `Navigation.app.*` and `Navigation.settings.*` keys; values from prototype labels]
- `apps/web/i18n/pl.json` [modify — same keys translated]

Do NOT modify proxy.ts, [locale]/layout.tsx, or any T-XXX.json outside this wave.

## Contract
Groups must include exactly: `Core` (Dashboard, Settings), `Operations` (Planning, Production, Warehouse), `QA & Shipping` (Quality, Shipping), `Premium` (Technical, NPD, Finance, OEE, Maintenance). Routes use the post-T-133 topology (e.g. `/settings`, `/planning`, `/production`, etc.) and MUST be locale-relative (no `/en` hard-code; the consumer applies the locale).

Each item:
```ts
{ key: 'settings', i18n_key: 'Navigation.app.settings', route: '/settings', icon_token: 'cog', count_slot: null, permission_key: null, rbac_todo: 'gate by 02-settings/T-130 after RBAC enum lock' }
```

Settings sub-nav items match `window.SETTINGS_NAV` in the prototype (Users, Roles, Authorization, Invitations, Security, Profile, Schema, Reference, …) with the same group labels.

## Acceptance criteria
1. `APP_NAV_GROUPS` has exactly 4 groups in declared order with the 12 items above.
2. `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS` matches the prototype `SETTINGS_NAV` content 1:1 (item count, order, labels, icons, highlights).
3. Every item carries a unique `i18n_key` resolvable in both `en.json` and `pl.json`.
4. RTL/unit tests assert: total counts, key uniqueness, route uniqueness, no hard-coded locale prefix, `count_slot === null` on every item, `permission_key === null` on every item, and `rbac_todo` non-empty for every item that would later be RBAC-gated.
5. `pnpm --filter web test app-nav settings-nav -- --run` passes; `pnpm --filter web typecheck` passes; `pnpm --filter web lint` passes.

## Out of scope
- Rendering (UI-129 sidebar, UI-132 settings subnav).
- Permissions enum / RBAC (02-settings/T-130).
- Module-specific module sub-navs (PlanNav, ProdNav, WhNav, ...).
- Topbar links.

## RED test
Create `app-nav.test.ts` and `settings-nav.test.ts` first, failing because exports do not exist; then implement.

## Risk red lines
- Never hard-code a locale prefix into a route field.
- Never gate items here — emit `rbac_todo` and leave `permission_key: null`.
- Do not import this file from server-only modules without `'server-only'` consideration (data is plain JSON; safe everywhere).

## Closeout
Provide: acceptance_criteria_status, test_commands_and_results, changed_files, git_status, deviations_from_prototype (note any item label that differs from the literal prototype label and why).

# UI-129 — Foundation AppSidebar

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Implement `<AppSidebar/>` — the persistent 280px left rail used on every authenticated desktop route. Consumes UI-127 tokens and UI-128 navigation manifest. No business logic; pure presentation + `usePathname` for active state. Count badges and permission gates are slots only.

## Prototype source of truth
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:3-21` (`SSidebar`).
- Cross-ref structure: `technical/shell.jsx:3-22` (`TSidebar`), `warehouse/shell.jsx` (`PSidebar`), `production/shell.jsx`.
- Every prototype renders the same four groups (Core / Operations / QA & Shipping / Premium) with the same items; only the `.active` item changes per module.

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/components/shell/app-sidebar.tsx` [create — Client Component (`'use client'`) using `usePathname` from `next/navigation` for active state]
- `apps/web/components/shell/__tests__/app-sidebar.test.tsx` [create — RTL with `next-intl` and `next/navigation` mocks]

Do NOT modify any T-XXX.json outside this wave; do NOT modify `proxy.ts`; do NOT add `app-sidebar.tsx` to a layout in this task (UI-131 mounts it).

## Contract
```tsx
export function AppSidebar({
  locale,
  pathnameOverride,
}: { locale: string; pathnameOverride?: string }): JSX.Element
```

Behaviour:
- Reads `APP_NAV_GROUPS` from `apps/web/lib/navigation/app-nav.ts` (UI-128).
- Resolves labels with `useTranslations('Navigation.app')` from `next-intl` (or the equivalent locale fixture; do not hard-code Polish/English strings).
- Prefixes `route` with `/${locale}` when rendering `<Link/>`.
- Active state when `pathname === /${locale}${item.route}` or starts with `/${locale}${item.route}/`.
- Renders `count_slot` as `null` for now (UI-128 sets it to null) but the JSX must include the `<span data-slot="count"/>` markup so modules can later inject content via React context or a portal.
- Each item carries `data-testid="app-sidebar-item-<key>"`.
- Root carries `data-testid="app-sidebar"`, `role="navigation"`, `aria-label="Primary"`.
- Width = `w-sidebar` token from UI-127. Background/foreground/border colours = UI-127 tokens.
- Each `rbac_todo` non-null item is rendered (no gating) and includes a JSX comment `{/* TODO(rbac/02-settings/T-130) */}`.

## Acceptance criteria
1. Render asserts: 4 group headers, 12 items, in the order from UI-128.
2. With `pathnameOverride='/en/settings/users'`, `data-testid="app-sidebar-item-settings"` has `aria-current="page"`; no other item does.
3. Each item is an `<a href="/en/<route>">` (Next `<Link/>`).
4. axe-core has 0 violations on the rendered component (`@axe-core/react` or `vitest-axe`).
5. `pnpm --filter web test app-sidebar -- --run` passes, typecheck passes, lint passes.
6. Tokens consumed: assert via `getComputedStyle` that root width === `var(--shell-sidebar-w)`.

## Out of scope
- Mounting in any layout (UI-131).
- Topbar/UserMenu (UI-130).
- Settings sub-nav (UI-132).
- Count badge live values (each module).
- Permission gating (02-settings/T-130).
- Mobile collapse / hamburger.

## RED test
Write `app-sidebar.test.tsx` first; it must fail because the component file does not exist.

## Risk red lines
- Do not import `APP_NAV_GROUPS` from anywhere except `apps/web/lib/navigation/app-nav.ts`.
- Do not hard-code locale prefix logic outside the explicit `/${locale}` join.
- Do not gate items by permissions.
- Do not mount in a layout — UI-131 owns mounting.

## Closeout (fail-closed)
Provide: screenshots desktop-1280 + 1440, screenshot_or_dom_diff_artifacts against prototype `settings/shell.jsx:3-21`, visual_parity_evidence, prototype_reference, console_network_check (no console errors), acceptance_criteria_status, test_commands_and_results (vitest output), changed_files, git_status, deviations_from_prototype. `ui_parity_evidence_missing` blocks closeout.

Tokens via w-sidebar/bg-shell-* utilities from UI-127. RTL tests with next-intl + next/navigation mocks. axe-core 0 violations. Do not mount in a layout (UI-131 mounts).

# UI-131 — Foundation (app) layout Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation). Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`. ## Goal Replace the T-133 pass-through stub at `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/layout.tsx` with the real AppShell grid (Sidebar + Topbar + main) and add a Server-Component defence-in-depth auth check. Edge auth in `proxy.ts` stays primary; this layout is the second gate and the shell host for every authenticated desktop route. ## Source - Brief `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md` §3 Luka A + Luka G. - Prototype layout: sidebar 280px + topbar 56px + main content; settings/shell.jsx is the canonical layout shape. ## Files (scope) - `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/layout.tsx` [modify — replace stub] - `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/__tests__/layout.test.tsx` [create — RTL for auth guard branches] - `apps/web/e2e/app-shell-layout.spec.ts` [create — Playwright smoke: assert sidebar + topbar appear on `/en/`] - `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/loading.tsx` [create — minimal shell skeleton, sidebar + topbar widths preserved, content area `

{children}

# UI-132 — Foundation PageHeader primitive + Settings subnav layout

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Deliver the shared `PageHeader` primitive used by every module page AND the Settings subnav layout that mounts under `(app)/(settings)/`. The brief calls these the same shell-frame deliverable (Lukas D + E) and `SettingsSubNav` consumes `PageHeader` for its actions row, so they ship together.

## Prototype source of truth
- PageHeader: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:61-69` (`PageHead`); cross-ref `oee/shell.jsx` PageHead.
- SettingsSubNav: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:35-59` (`SettingsNav`); uses the `SETTINGS_NAV` array transcribed in UI-128.

## Files (scope)
- `packages/ui/src/PageHeader.tsx` [create — Server-Component-safe presentational primitive, no `'use client'`]
- `packages/ui/src/__tests__/PageHeader.test.tsx` [create — RTL fixture, axe-core 0 violations]
- `packages/ui/src/index.ts` [modify — re-export `PageHeader` + `PageHeaderProps`]
- `apps/web/components/shell/settings-subnav.tsx` [create — Client Component (`'use client'`) consuming `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS` from UI-128, `usePathname` for active state]
- `apps/web/components/shell/__tests__/settings-subnav.test.tsx` [create]
- `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(admin)/settings/layout.tsx` [create — Server Component that wraps `{children}` with a two-column shell: 240px subnav + main; uses UI-127 token `--shell-subnav-w`]

Do NOT modify `proxy.ts`, `[locale]/layout.tsx`, `[locale]/(app)/layout.tsx` (UI-131), `user-menu-language-picker.tsx`, or any T-XXX.json outside this wave.

## Contract
```tsx
// packages/ui/src/PageHeader.tsx
export interface PageHeaderProps {
  title: string;
  subtitle?: string;
  breadcrumb?: ReactNode;
  actions?: ReactNode;
}
export function PageHeader(props: PageHeaderProps): JSX.Element;
```
Layout: optional `breadcrumb` row above; `title` (`h1`) + optional `subtitle` (`p`) on the left; `actions` slot pinned right. Root carries `data-testid="page-header"`; title carries `data-testid="page-header-title"`; actions carry `data-testid="page-header-actions"`.

```tsx
// apps/web/components/shell/settings-subnav.tsx (Client)
export interface SettingsSubNavProps { locale: string; pathnameOverride?: string }
export function SettingsSubNav(props: SettingsSubNavProps): JSX.Element;
```
Reads `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS` from `apps/web/lib/navigation/settings-nav.ts` (UI-128). Renders each item as a Next `<Link/>` to `/${locale}${item.route}`. Active when `pathname === /${locale}${item.route}` or starts with `/${locale}${item.route}/`. Each item carries `data-testid="settings-subnav-item-<key>"`. Root carries `data-testid="settings-subnav"`, `role="navigation"`, `aria-label="Settings"`. Width = `w-subnav` (UI-127). Each `rbac_todo` item is rendered with a JSX comment `{/* TODO(rbac/02-settings/T-130) */}` and `data-todo="rbac/02-settings/T-130"`; no gating.

```tsx
// apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(admin)/settings/layout.tsx (Server Component)
export default async function SettingsLayout({
  children, params,
}: { children: ReactNode; params: Promise<{ locale: string }> }): Promise<JSX.Element>
```
Renders a CSS grid `grid-cols-[var(--shell-subnav-w)_1fr]` with `<SettingsSubNav locale={locale}/>` and `<main data-testid="settings-main">{children}</main>`. No auth check (the parent `(app)/layout.tsx` from UI-131 already does it).

## Acceptance criteria
1. `import { PageHeader } from '@monopilot/ui'` resolves; `PageHeaderProps` is exported.
2. PageHeader renders title (`h1`), optional subtitle (`p`), optional breadcrumb above, optional actions right-aligned; axe-core 0 violations.
3. `SettingsSubNav` renders every group/item from `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS` in declared order; active item carries `aria-current="page"`; non-active items do not.
4. `(settings)/layout.tsx` puts the subnav in a 240px column and `{children}` in the remaining column; visiting `/en/settings`, `/en/settings/users`, `/en/settings/roles` shows the subnav and a settings-main region.
5. Visiting `/en/settings` from a non-settings sibling (e.g. `/en/`) shows no `settings-subnav` — verified by a Playwright spec snippet inside `apps/web/e2e/settings-subnav.spec.ts`.
6. All RTL tests pass: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui test PageHeader -- --run`, `pnpm --filter web test settings-subnav -- --run`.
7. `pnpm --filter web typecheck && pnpm --filter web lint && pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui typecheck && pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui lint` pass.
8. `apps/web/proxy.ts` and `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/layout.tsx` are byte-identical before/after.

## Out of scope
- AppSidebar / AppTopbar (UI-129/130).
- (app)/layout.tsx grid (UI-131).
- Scanner shell (T-134).
- RBAC gating (02-settings/T-130).
- Module-specific subnavs (PlanNav, ProdNav, …).
- Mobile responsive collapse.

## RED test
Write `PageHeader.test.tsx`, `settings-subnav.test.tsx`, and `e2e/settings-subnav.spec.ts` first — they must fail because the files do not exist.

## Risk red lines
- PageHeader must NOT be marked `'use client'` — it is consumed by Server Component module pages.
- `SettingsSubNav` is `'use client'` (needs `usePathname`); the `(settings)/layout.tsx` itself stays Server Component.
- Do not gate items by permissions; emit `data-todo="rbac/02-settings/T-130"`.
- Do not duplicate the nav array — only import `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS` from UI-128.
- Do not modify `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/layout.tsx` (UI-131 owns it) or `proxy.ts`.

## Closeout (fail-closed)
Provide: screenshots desktop-1280 + 1440 for `/en/settings` and `/en/settings/users` and the PageHeader fixture, screenshot_or_dom_diff_artifacts against prototype `settings/shell.jsx:35-59` and `:61-69`, visual_parity_evidence, prototype_reference, console_network_check (no errors, no failed requests, no hydration warnings), acceptance_criteria_status, test_commands_and_results, changed_files, git_status, deviations_from_prototype. `ui_parity_evidence_missing` blocks closeout.

## IA decision (binding)
Settings pages for this wave live under `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(admin)/settings/*`, matching the existing `(admin)/settings/users` topology after T-133. Do not create a parallel `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(settings)/*` settings layout.

# T-133 — Foundation route topology refactor + contract

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Reorganise `apps/web/app/[locale]/` into three sibling route groups — `(app)`, `(auth)`, `(scanner)` — without changing any URL, so UI-131 can attach the AppShell to `(app)` only, login keeps its own card layout under `(auth)`, and `(scanner)` is wired before T-134 lands the ScannerFrame. Also produce the contract test that proves no 404 / no wrong redirect after the move.

## Source
Brief `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md` §3 Luka A; §7 risks (login-renders-sidebar, scanner-inherits-shell, reorg-breaks-tests). `apps/web/proxy.ts` already centralises auth/idle/i18n at the edge — DO NOT move auth into a Node middleware here.

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/layout.tsx` [create — placeholder Server Component that just returns `<>{children}</>`; UI-131 fills it with the AppShell + auth guard]
- `apps/web/app/[locale]/(auth)/layout.tsx` [create — empty pass-through; login keeps its own visual layout]
- `apps/web/app/[locale]/(scanner)/layout.tsx` [create — empty pass-through; T-134 fills it with ScannerFrame]
- relocate: `apps/web/app/[locale]/login/` → `apps/web/app/[locale]/(auth)/login/`
- relocate: `apps/web/app/[locale]/page.tsx` → `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/page.tsx`
- relocate: `apps/web/app/(admin)/` → `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(admin)/` (URLs stay because route groups are URL-transparent; verify with the contract test)
- relocate: `apps/web/app/(npd)/` → `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(npd)/`
- relocate (if exists): any `apps/web/app/(settings)/` → `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(settings)/`
- `apps/web/e2e/route-topology.spec.ts` [create — Playwright contract test, see below]
- Update import paths only where module-relative imports break.

Do NOT modify `proxy.ts`, `[locale]/layout.tsx` (NextIntlProvider stays), `[locale]/login/layout.tsx` content (move-only), any `T-XXX.json` outside this wave, or `00-foundation/manifest.json`.

## URL contract (assertions for the spec)
Every URL below MUST resolve with HTTP 200 (or 302→login for unauth) BOTH before and after the refactor:

- `GET /en/` → before-after: same status, same redirect chain
- `GET /en/login` → 200
- `GET /en/login/forgot-password` → 200
- `GET /en/login/mfa?factorId=red-contract` → 200
- `GET /en/settings`, `/en/settings/users`, `/en/settings/roles`, `/en/settings/profile`, `/en/settings/security`, `/en/settings/invitations`, `/en/settings/authorization`, `/en/settings/schema/preview`, `/en/settings/reference/manufacturing-operations` → 200 (auth or DEV_AUTH_BYPASS)
- `GET /en/account/profile`, `/en/account/notifications` → 200 (auth or DEV_AUTH_BYPASS)
- `GET /en/fa/<productCode>` → 200
- `GET /pl/login` → 200 (locale mirror)

Not covered here:
- `(scanner)` routes don't exist yet (T-134 introduces them). The contract test only asserts the empty `(scanner)/layout.tsx` exists.

## Auth-guard placement
Auth guard stays in `proxy.ts` (existing). UI-131 will additionally call `createServerSupabaseClient().auth.getUser()` inside `(app)/layout.tsx` as a defence-in-depth Server-Component check — that's a UI-131 deliverable, not T-133.

## Acceptance criteria
1. Each of the 18+ URLs above returns the same status before and after relocation (proven by running the new spec twice in the same run — once against snapshot URLs, once against the relocated tree).
2. Existing Playwright specs (`apps/web/e2e/auth.spec.ts`, `apps/web/e2e/login-ui-parity.spec.ts`) pass unchanged.
3. Existing vitest suite (`pnpm --filter web test`) passes unchanged — no test file is renamed/moved, only `app/` subtrees move.
4. Typecheck passes (`pnpm --filter web typecheck`).
5. `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/layout.tsx`, `(auth)/layout.tsx`, `(scanner)/layout.tsx` exist as pass-through stubs; the file headers cite the wave + this task id and reference the follow-on tasks (UI-131 / T-134).
6. proxy.ts is NOT modified.

## Out of scope
- AppShell wrapper content (UI-131).
- AuthGuard in Server Component (UI-131).
- ScannerFrame (T-134).
- New URLs / removed URLs / locale changes.

## RED test
Write `route-topology.spec.ts` and run against the pre-refactor tree first — capture baseline statuses. Then perform the relocation, run again, diff. The committed spec asserts the post-refactor contract; the baseline run is referenced in closeout.

## Risk red lines
- Do not move `proxy.ts` or change its matcher.
- Do not change any URL externally — route groups are URL-transparent.
- Do not delete the existing `[locale]/login/layout.tsx` content; only relocate it.
- Do not rename any test file (would alter coverage and blame).
- Do not modify `_meta/atomic-tasks/00-foundation/manifest.json` or any task under `tasks/T-001..T-126`.

## Closeout
Provide: changed_files (with `git mv` history preserved), test_commands_and_results for both baseline and post-refactor runs, acceptance_criteria_status, git_status, deviations_from_prototype (n/a — pure structural), and the route-topology diff table.

If any URL changes status across the refactor, the task is BLOCKED — do not promote.

## Existing-tree inventory and collision policy (binding)
Before moving anything, inventory the current App Router tree with `git status --short apps/web/app` and a file listing for these known collision/risk areas:
- `apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/users/**`
- `apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/users/**`
- `apps/web/app/(auth)/actions.ts`
- `apps/web/app/(settings)/schema/_actions/**`
- `apps/web/app/onboarding/**` special routes

IA decision for this task:
- Final authenticated shell group is `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)`.
- Final settings pages live at `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(admin)/settings/*`; do not create or keep a parallel `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(settings)/*` settings UI tree.
- Existing duplicate settings/users implementations must be compared and merged surgically. Do not pretend this is a simple `git mv`; do not overwrite either side without preserving tests, route contracts, and imports.
- Preserve `apps/web/app/(auth)/actions.ts` behaviour/imports unless inventory proves it must move; update imports explicitly if it moves.
- Treat `apps/web/app/(settings)/schema/_actions/**` as action support code, not the settings subnav layout target. Preserve or relocate only after import inventory proves the safe destination.
- Onboarding routes are special: leave them outside the AppShell unless the inventory/contract explicitly proves they are authenticated shell routes; document the decision in closeout.

Document the move in closeout with inventory, collision decisions, and git history.

# T-135 — Foundation shell unit contracts (RTL)

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Consolidate the per-component RTL specs created by UI-129/130/131/132 and T-134 into a single contract suite that asserts the cross-component invariants the brief defines as KRYTYCZNE. Each invariant is one focused spec; together they form the shell unit gate that runs before the Playwright gate (T-136). This task does NOT replace the per-component specs — it adds the cross-component contracts that no single UI task owns.

## Source
Brief `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md` §7 risks (scanner inherits AppShell, login renders sidebar, SiteSwitcher too early, T-130 not done when UI-132 ready, language picker `'use client'`).

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/components/shell/__tests__/shell-contract.test.tsx` [create — RTL suite covering the cross-component invariants below]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/__tests__/shell-nav-integrity.test.ts` [create — pure-data integrity assertions on UI-128 manifests as they appear at runtime]

Do NOT create new product code in this task. Do NOT modify any UI-127..UI-132 / T-133 / T-134 source file. Do NOT modify proxy.ts or any T-XXX.json outside this wave.

## Invariants to assert (the suite)
1. AppShell composition — rendering `(app)/layout.tsx` (with a mocked authed Supabase client + mocked `next/headers`) yields exactly one `data-testid="app-shell"`, exactly one `app-sidebar`, exactly one `app-topbar`, and children are rendered under `app-shell-main`.
2. Login isolation — rendering the `(auth)/login/page.tsx` tree must NOT contain any of `app-shell`, `app-sidebar`, `app-topbar`, `settings-subnav`, `scanner-frame` test-ids.
3. Settings composition — rendering `(app)/(admin)/settings/layout.tsx` with mocked authed session yields `app-shell` AND `settings-subnav` AND a `settings-main` region; the active settings item carries `aria-current="page"`.
4. Scanner isolation — rendering `(scanner)/layout.tsx` yields `scanner-frame` and contains NONE of `app-shell`/`app-sidebar`/`app-topbar`/`settings-subnav`.
5. SiteCrumb static slot — `<SiteCrumb orgName="Acme"/>` renders text `Acme`, carries `data-todo="multi-site-T-020"`, and contains no `role="combobox"` / `role="listbox"` / `<select>` / Radix Select primitives.
6. RBAC TODO — every `settings-subnav` item in the rendered tree carries `data-todo="rbac/02-settings/T-130"`; no item is conditionally hidden by a permission check.
7. Language picker mount boundary — rendering `<UserMenu>` mounts `UserMenuLanguagePicker` exactly once; `UserMenuLanguagePicker` source file has not been modified (assert via `fs.readFileSync` hash comparison stored as a frozen baseline in the test).
8. Tokens consumed — for each of `app-sidebar`, `app-topbar`, `settings-subnav`, `scanner-frame`, the suite asserts the relevant `getComputedStyle` width/height resolves to the UI-127 CSS variable value (not a literal px elsewhere). The CSS-var values themselves come from `apps/web/app/globals.css` parsed at test time.
9. Nav manifest integrity — `APP_NAV_GROUPS` has 4 groups and 12 items; all `route` values are locale-relative (start with `/`, do not start with `/en` or `/pl`); every `i18n_key` resolves in both `apps/web/i18n/en.json` and `pl.json`; `count_slot === null` and `permission_key === null` on every item; `rbac_todo` is a non-empty string on every item that the brief earmarks for RBAC gating.
10. Settings nav manifest integrity — same shape as (9) for `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS`; route uniqueness; key uniqueness; i18n resolution in both locales.

## Acceptance criteria
1. All 10 invariants implemented as discrete `it(...)` cases under either `shell-contract.test.tsx` (rendering) or `shell-nav-integrity.test.ts` (pure data).
2. Each invariant case has a comment naming the brief §7 risk or §3 luka it guards.
3. The suite is RED before UI-129..UI-132 + T-134 land and GREEN after they all land. Pipeline must run T-135 after each of those is closed; this is enforced by the wave DAG (priority 85, depends on UI-129, UI-130, UI-131, UI-132, T-134).
4. `pnpm --filter web test shell-contract shell-nav-integrity -- --run` passes.
5. `pnpm --filter web typecheck` and `lint` pass.
6. No file outside `apps/web/components/shell/__tests__/` and `apps/web/lib/navigation/__tests__/` is modified.

## Out of scope
- Browser-level (Playwright) assertions — T-136 owns them.
- Visual regression / screenshot diffing.
- Per-component specs that already exist in UI-129..UI-132/T-134 (do NOT duplicate them).
- Mocking Supabase / next/headers beyond what the tests need to render.

## RED test
The suite is itself the RED test for the wave; commit it before promoting UI-131..T-134, then re-run after.

## Risk red lines
- Do not import production code from `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/` directly into Vitest fixtures without mocking `next/headers`, `next/navigation`, `next-intl`, and `createServerSupabaseClient` — they require the App Router runtime otherwise.
- Do not fork the navigation manifest in tests — import the real modules from UI-128.
- Do not assert pixel-perfect visual diffs here (T-136).
- Do not modify product source files; only test files.

## Closeout
Provide: test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, changed_files (only the two test files), git_status, deviations_from_prototype (n/a — pure unit contracts).

# T-136 — Foundation AppShell browser error-discovery spec

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Deliver the wave's final parity gate: a Playwright spec that visits every shell-relevant route in a real browser, fails closed on the listed categories of browser errors, and writes a machine-readable `parity_report.json` with `recommended_followups[]` that a future Opus run can convert into ACP tasks. This task DOES NOT create ACP tasks itself — it only writes the report and lets the operator decide.

## Source
Brief `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md` §3 Luka H + §8 (F-AS-T07 contract) + final Spark-style audit summary ("Browser gate must fail on pageerror/console.error/requestfailed/404/500 and produce follow-up evidence"). The brief's §8 originally said `error_discovery_mode: true` / non-failing WARN; this wave OVERRIDES that — the spec MUST fail closed on the listed categories. The user-facing report still carries the `recommended_followups[]` array for human triage.

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/e2e/shell-smoke.spec.ts` [create — Playwright spec; uses the project's auth-storage fixture from T-123]
- `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/.gitkeep` [create]
- `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/parity_report.schema.json` [create — JSON Schema describing the report shape so consumers (Opus, dashboards) can parse it deterministically]
- `apps/web/e2e/_helpers/shell-parity.ts` [create — helpers: `installConsoleSpy(page)`, `installNetworkSpy(page)`, `assertShellRegions(page, expected)`, `buildReportEntry(...)`, `writeReport(reportPath, entries)`]

Do NOT modify `apps/web/proxy.ts`, any AppShell product file, or any T-XXX.json outside this wave. Do NOT shell out to ACP or create tasks programmatically.

## Routes visited (per spec)
| URL | Auth state | expects_shell | expects_subnav | active_nav_item |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `/en/` | authed | true | none | `dashboard` |
| `/en/settings` | authed | true | `settings` | `settings` |
| `/en/settings/users` | authed | true | `settings` | `settings` (sidebar) + `users` (subnav) |
| `/en/settings/roles` | authed | true | `settings` | `settings` (sidebar) + `roles` (subnav) |
| `/en/login` | unauthed | false | none | n/a |
| `/en/dev/scanner` | authed (scanner harness — auth model TBD by scanner module) | false (ScannerFrame instead) | none | n/a |
| `/pl/login` | unauthed | false | none | n/a (locale mirror) |

## Failure conditions (the spec FAILS the run if ANY of these fire on ANY route)
1. `page.on('pageerror', ...)` — any uncaught JS exception.
2. `page.on('console', msg => msg.type() === 'error')` — including hydration warnings: any console message whose text matches `/Warning: Text content did not match/i` OR `/Hydration failed/i` OR `/Did not expect server HTML/i`.
3. `page.on('requestfailed', ...)` — any failed request (network errors, blocked, aborted).
4. Any response with status `>= 400` (404, 500, etc.) is a failure, EXCEPT when the route's row in the table above has an expected redirect (none of the listed routes do — `/en/login` returns 200 directly).
5. For rows with `expects_shell: true`: missing landmark/region — any of `[data-testid="app-shell"]`, `[data-testid="app-sidebar"]`, `[data-testid="app-topbar"]`, or no `<main>` element, or no `<nav aria-label="Primary">`.
6. For rows with `expects_shell: false`: presence of any of `[data-testid="app-shell"]`, `[data-testid="app-sidebar"]`, or `[data-testid="app-topbar"]` in the response body.
7. Wrong active nav: for rows with `active_nav_item`, that sidebar item must carry `aria-current="page"` AND no other sidebar item may. Same rule for subnav items where applicable.
8. Wrong localized redirect: visiting `/login` (no locale prefix) MUST redirect to `/${default_locale}/login`; the spec asserts the chain and fails on a missing redirect, a 4xx/5xx during the chain, or a redirect to the wrong locale.

Each failure is recorded in `parity_report.json` AND fails the spec.

## Report contract
Write `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/parity_report.json`:
```json
{
  "wave": "app-shell-foundation-20260520",
  "task": "T-136",
  "generated_at": "<ISO-8601>",
  "playwright_version": "<from package.json>",
  "entries": [
    {
      "route": "/en/settings/users",
      "auth": "authed",
      "expects_shell": true,
      "expects_subnav": "settings",
      "active_nav_item": {"sidebar": "settings", "subnav": "users"},
      "screenshot_path": "apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/en-settings-users-desktop-1440.png",
      "console_errors": [{"text": "...", "location": "..."}],
      "hydration_warnings": ["..."],
      "page_errors": ["..."],
      "failed_requests": [{"url": "...", "failure": "..."}],
      "bad_status_responses": [{"url": "...", "status": 500}],
      "missing_regions": ["app-sidebar"],
      "wrong_active_nav": null,
      "wrong_localized_redirect": null,
      "result": "FAIL"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {"pass": 0, "fail": 7, "warn": 0},
  "recommended_followups": [
    {
      "kind": "task_proposal",
      "display_id_hint": "UI-???",
      "category": "ui",
      "reason": "app-sidebar missing on /en/settings/users — UI-131 mount likely broken",
      "evidence_routes": ["/en/settings/users"],
      "prototype_refs": ["prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:3-21"],
      "note": "NOT auto-created. Operator/Opus reviews and decides whether to file via TaskCreate."
    }
  ]
}
```
The schema in `parity_report.schema.json` is the canonical shape.

Follow-up rules:
- Each unique failure category per route produces one `recommended_followups[]` entry.
- The spec MUST NOT call any ACP API, MUST NOT shell out to `kira_dev`, MUST NOT write outside `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/`.
- The spec MUST set `"note": "NOT auto-created. ..."` on every entry.

## Acceptance criteria
1. `apps/web/e2e/shell-smoke.spec.ts` exists, runs under `pnpm exec playwright test e2e/shell-smoke.spec.ts`.
2. The spec exits non-zero if any failure condition (1–8 above) fires on any visited route.
3. The spec writes `parity_report.json` AND a per-route full-page screenshot under `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/`.
4. The report validates against `parity_report.schema.json` (assert with `ajv` inside the spec teardown or a separate vitest unit, your choice).
5. The report contains `recommended_followups[]` populated only on failures; on a fully-passing run the array is empty but present.
6. No ACP task is created by this spec. No file outside `apps/web/e2e/` is modified by the run.
7. `apps/web/proxy.ts` and AppShell components are byte-identical before/after.
8. Locally and in CI the spec is wired into `pnpm exec playwright test` (i.e. picked up by the default test glob).

## Out of scope
- Auto-creating ACP tasks from the report (deliberately a human / Opus decision step).
- Visual regression tooling beyond a screenshot dump.
- Cross-browser matrix (use the project default Playwright browser only).
- Mobile viewports (deferred wave).
- Modifying `proxy.ts`, AppShell product code, or any other task JSON.

## RED test
The spec is itself the RED gate. Run it once before promoting UI-131/UI-132/T-134 (every route fails); run it after they all land (every route should pass).

## Risk red lines
- Do NOT mark `error_discovery_mode: true` here — the wave OVERRIDES the brief's original WARN-only stance; this gate MUST fail closed.
- Do NOT call ACP APIs or create tasks from this spec.
- Do NOT write outside `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/`.
- Do NOT modify the AppShell components when fixing failures uncovered by this spec — file follow-up tasks instead.
- Do NOT modify `apps/web/proxy.ts`.

## Closeout (fail-closed)
Provide: test_commands_and_results (Playwright run output), parity_report.json (committed under `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/`), per-route screenshots (also committed), acceptance_criteria_status, changed_files, git_status, deviations_from_prototype, console_network_check (the report IS the check), screenshots, screenshot_or_dom_diff_artifacts, visual_parity_evidence, prototype_reference. `ui_parity_evidence_missing` blocks closeout.
